channel.title: Preventative
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Preventative, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: These 3 Types Of People Should Definitely Take A Probiotic
item.description: Of all the supplements out there, probiotics earn a spot toward the top of the priority list.

item.title: Stop Your Parenting Shame Spiral—Helpful Advice From A Mom & Therapist
item.description: Parenting is hard, but shame is not required.

item.title: Journalists Explain Colorectal Cancer Risk and Federal Pullback on Georgia Disability Oversight
item.description: KFF Health News journalists made the rounds on national and local media recently to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of their appearances.

item.title: I Study Cognition & This Is How To Know How "Old" Your Brain Is
item.description: Plus, how to maintain a "younger" brain.

item.title: Eat This Nut Daily To Decrease Your Risk Of Cognitive Decline (No, Not Brazil Nuts)
item.description: Walnuts hold health benefits from your head to your toes.

item.title: This Week's Chaotic Horoscope Brings Mercury Retrograde & More
item.description: Mercury is retrograding through Pisces.

item.title: When It Comes to Health Insurance, Federal Dollars Support More Than ACA Plans
item.description: Questions of fairness came up in last year’s congressional debate about extending Obamacare’s enhanced subsidies. Critics wondered why the federal gov...

item.title: Should Drug Companies Be Advertising to Consumers?
item.description: Aging means “becoming a target” of the industry, one expert said. After decades of debate, politicians of all stripes are proposing bans.

item.title: State Lawmakers Seek Restraints on Wage Garnishment for Medical Debt
item.description: At least eight states are considering legislation to curtail wage garnishment over unpaid medical bills, as health care costs rise and more people bec...

item.title: Trump’s Transparent Hospital Pricing Pays Off for Industry — But Not So Much for Patients
item.description: Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

item.title: Can We Ride the GenAI Wave Without Getting Subsumed by It?
item.description: By DAVID SHAYWITZ “There are decades where nothing happens; and there are weeks where decades happen,” said Lenin, probably never. It’s also a remarka...

item.title: High Blood Pressure 101
item.description: February is American Heart Month . Almost 1 in 2 adults in the United States have high blood pressure, but only half of those people — about 1 in 4 — ...

item.title: Are Protein Bars Actually Good for You?
item.description: Protein bars have become the go-to snack for busy women trying to eat healthier. They're tucked into gym bags, desk drawers and carry-on luggage, prom...

item.title: Medical Tourism 101
item.description: Millions of people travel to a different country for medical care every year. My friend Ann* is one of them. My first response when I heard she wanted...

item.title: Reverend Jesse Jackson, a Civil Rights Legend, Has Passed Away
item.description: The Reverend Jesse Louis Jackson, Sr., who worked with Dr. Martin Luther King, founded the Rainbow PUSH Coalition and ran twice for the Democratic nom...

item.title: The Body Knows: Two Mothers Who Survived the Unthinkable
item.description: Motherhood is often framed as a new chapter, a deepening of identity, and the arrival of a love unlike any other. For two women, Anjellica Davis and E...

item.title: Sofia Noori, Nema Health
item.description: Nema Health has cracked the code on PTSD and has an amazing study showing that their technique of intensive cognitive processing therapy is over 99% e...

item.title: AI replaces me: The Private AI Bubble Meets the Public Digital Health Reality Check
item.description: By CLAUDE.AI trying to write like me MATTHEW HOLT I finally did what I have been wanting to do for a while and had an AI ingest everything I’ve writte...

item.title: Amodei Believes “AI-Enabled Autocracy” Could Be Imminent
item.description: By MIKE MAGEE Last month, Dario Amodei, CEO of Anthropic, did it again – scaring the bejesus out of societal leaders worldwide with warnings that thei...

item.title: ¿Qué efectos tiene el lupus en la salud sexual y reproductiva?
item.description: English Hay varios tipos de lupus, pero las personas frecuentemente usan la palabra "lupus" para referirse al lupus eritematoso sistémico (LES), el ti...

item.title: Lupus and Reproductive Health: What You Need to Know
item.description: Español There are several types of lupus, but people often use the word “lupus” to mean systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), the most common form of th...

item.title: Ten Ten Ten – Trying to radically fix health care in the US
item.description: The goal of the new non profit Ten Ten Ten founded by TJ Tedesco & Bhargav Raman is to take America from being the most expensive health system in the...

item.title: Where Women Live Impacts Their Health: Here Are The 10 Best States
item.description: Where a woman lives plays a critical role in her health outcomes. It shapes her access to healthcare, safety, and overall quality of life. A new analy...

item.title: 10 Things Black and Brown Women Need to Know About Heart Disease
item.description: Heart disease is the leading cause of death for Black and Brown women—yet many don’t know their unique risks. These ten facts highlight what every wom...

item.title: Kid ‘n Play‘s Christopher Reid Reveals He Had a Heart Transplant
item.description: In an interview with Michael Strahan on “GMA” on February 5th, Christopher “Kid” Reid, of the rap duo Kid ‘n Play and co-star of the House Party movie...
